title: Mildew on Upholstery
timeEstimate: 30–90 minutes home triage; structural mildew = remediation specialist, not a stain job
stainChemistry: Mildew on upholstery is a living fungal colony — hyphae penetrate fiber and foam, spores release on contact, metabolic byproducts cause gray/black/green staining, and microbial volatile organic compounds cause the musty odor. Surface mildew on a removable, washable cushion cover is a tractable home job. Structural mildew — fungus in the foam, batting, or frame — is not a stain; it is mold contamination, and the right move is a remediation specialist, not a peroxide bottle. Care code matters: W (water-safe) tolerates the home protocol, S (solvent-only) does not, WS allows both, X is brush/vacuum only. Wool blend, silk blend, vintage upholstery, or unknown care code = pro from minute one. Over-wetting saturates foam and creates a second mildew problem inside the cushion.
whyThisWorks: OxiClean / sodium percarbonate at cool temperature on water-safe upholstery kills surface mildew organisms and oxidizes pigmented byproducts without the chlorine-bleach risks (color-stripping, fiber damage, foam degradation). Sun-dry adds UV antimicrobial finish and helps drive residual moisture out of foam. The hard prerequisite: fix the moisture source. Mildew returns to the same spot if humidity, leak, or condensation is unresolved. Without the moisture fix, every cleaning is temporary.
defaultAssumption: Care-code W or WS upholstery with surface mildew on a removable cover or accessible fabric, fresh-to-weeks-old, in a known damp location (basement, garage, bathroom-adjacent). Structural mildew, foam saturation, S/X care codes, vintage, wool, or silk = pro / remediation specialist. Removable washable covers handle better than fixed upholstery.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Good light, care tag, N95 mask, gloves
spottingProtocol.instruction: Find the care tag. W or WS = home protocol candidate. S, X, unknown, vintage, wool, silk = pro. Press the cushion. Foam squishes back to dry = surface only. Foam stays compressed or smells musty inside = structural mildew, stop and call remediation. PPE: N95 + gloves before touching mildew.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Soft brush, HEPA vacuum, outdoor space
spottingProtocol.instruction: Take the cushion outdoors. Soft-brush loose spores away from yourself, downwind. HEPA vacuum follows the brush. Never indoors — spores aerosolize and recolonize.

spottingProtocol.equipment: OxiClean per label, COOL water, white cloth, soft brush
spottingProtocol.instruction: Mix OxiClean per label in COOL water. Apply with damp cloth — do NOT pour or saturate. Soft-brush gently. 15–20 min dwell. Cool damp-cloth rinse 2–3 passes. Foam saturation = bigger mildew problem inside.
White or near-white water-safe upholstery only. Hidden test mandatory on dyed fabric. 3% peroxide damp cloth, 5–10 min, damp-cloth rinse. Skip on dyed, vintage, wool, silk, or any S/X care code.

spottingProtocol.dwellTime: Half-day minimum, multi-day for thick cushions
spottingProtocol.instruction: Direct sunlight on the treated area for several hours. UV kills residual spores and drives moisture out of foam. Cushion must be fully dry inside before reuse — fan-dry indoors after sun. Damp foam = mildew returns within days.

Identify and fix the moisture source — humidity above 60%, basement leak, condensation, wet-room adjacency. Without this, mildew returns to the same spot. Dehumidifier to under 50% RH, repair leaks, add ventilation.

steps: 1. Care code and structural assessment. Surface vs structural fork.
2. PPE up. Outdoor HEPA vacuum and brush spore removal.
3. Controlled OxiClean / dilute oxygen bleach application, cool, damp not saturated.
4. 3% H₂O₂ spot pass on whites and light fabrics with hidden test.
5. Sun-dry UV finish plus full-cushion moisture extraction.
products: 3% Hydrogen peroxide (whites and light fabrics, hidden test)
professionalProtocol.temperature: cool throughout — heat sets pigmented byproducts and stresses foam
warnings: Structural mildew (foam, batting, frame) = remediation specialist, not a stain job
S, X, unknown care codes, vintage, wool, silk = pro from minute one
Never saturate the cushion — foam mildew is worse than surface
Never chlorine bleach — color-strips dyed fabric, damages many synthetics, degrades foam
PPE required — mildew spores aerosolize and are inhalation hazards
Moisture-source fix is non-negotiable for durability

homeSolutions.agent: Mildew Is a Living Organism, Not a Stain
homeSolutions.instruction: Mildew is fungus growing in your upholstery. Surface mildew on a washable cushion cover is fixable. Mildew inside the foam, batting, or frame is mold contamination — that is a remediation job, not a peroxide bottle. Press the cushion. If the foam smells musty inside or stays damp, stop here and call a specialist. Do not breathe spores. N95 mask and gloves before you start.

homeSolutions.agent: Check the Care Code Before Anything Else
homeSolutions.instruction: Find the care tag. W or WS means water-safe and you can proceed. S means solvent only — pro job. X means brush and vacuum only — pro. Vintage, wool, silk, or no tag = pro. Wrong care code + water = ruined upholstery.

Take the cushion outside. Soft-brush the loose mildew away from your face, downwind. Follow with a HEPA vacuum. Never do this indoors — spores spread and start growing in new spots. Wear the N95 the whole time.

homeSolutions.agent: OxiClean on a Damp Cloth — Do Not Saturate
homeSolutions.instruction: Mix OxiClean per label in COOL water. Apply with a damp cloth — do NOT pour and do NOT saturate. Wet foam grows new mildew inside the cushion. Soft-brush gently for 15 to 20 minutes. Damp-cloth rinse with cool water 2 to 3 passes.

WHITE or very light water-safe upholstery only: 3% drugstore peroxide on a damp cloth, 5 to 10 minutes, damp-cloth rinse. Hidden-area test first on anything dyed. Skip on dyed, vintage, wool, silk, or any S/X care code.

homeSolutions.agent: Sun-Dry Until Fully Dry — Inside and Out
homeSolutions.instruction: Direct sun on the treated area for several hours. UV finishes off residual spores and drives moisture out of the foam. Cushion must be fully dry inside before going back on the couch. Fan-dry afterward. Damp foam = mildew is back in days.

homeSolutions.agent: Fix the Moisture Source — This Is Not Optional
homeSolutions.instruction: Mildew returns to the same spot if the conditions return. Find the cause: basement humidity above 60%, leaky window, wet-room adjacency, condensation. Run a dehumidifier under 50% RH. Repair leaks. Improve ventilation. Without this, every cleaning is temporary.

steps: 1. Care code check. W or WS only. S, X, unknown, vintage, wool, silk = pro.
2. Press the cushion. Musty inside or wet foam = remediation specialist, not home.
3. PPE: N95 + gloves. Take cushion outdoors.
4. Soft-brush + HEPA vacuum spores away from face, downwind.
5. OxiClean cool damp-cloth — do NOT saturate. 15–20 min. Cool damp-cloth rinse.
6. 3% peroxide spot pass on white / light fabric only, hidden test.
7. Sun-dry several hours + fan-dry until foam is fully dry inside.
8. Fix the moisture source — dehumidifier, leak repair, ventilation.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if care code is S, X, unknown, vintage, wool, or silk. Stop if foam is damp, smells musty inside, or stays compressed — that is structural mildew. Stop if mildew returns within days — moisture source needs professional diagnosis. Stop after 2 home cycles without progress.
warnings: Structural mildew = remediation specialist, NOT home cleaning
S, X, unknown care codes, vintage, wool, silk = pro from minute one
Never saturate — wet foam grows new mildew inside
Never chlorine bleach — strips color, damages foam, doesn't fix the cause
PPE is required — mildew spores are an inhalation hazard

neverDo: Never treat S, X, or unknown-care-code upholstery at home
Never saturate upholstery with mildew solutions — foam mildew is worse
Never brush mildew indoors — spores aerosolize
Never skip PPE — N95 and gloves are required
fiberSensitivities: W (water-safe) and WS care codes: tolerate cool OxiClean damp-cloth method
S (solvent-only) and X (vacuum-only) care codes: pro from minute one
Wool / silk blend upholstery: pro — peroxide yellows wool, OxiClean damages silk
Vintage / antique upholstery: pro — fiber and stuffing degradation risk
Foam cushions: saturation creates structural mildew inside the cushion
Mixed-fiber upholstery: treat for the most sensitive fiber present

materialWarnings: Structural mildew is mold contamination — remediation specialist territory
Care code gates everything — W/WS only, otherwise pro
Foam saturation creates a worse mildew problem inside
Moisture-source fix is part of the protocol, not optional

escalation.when: Care code S, X, unknown, vintage, wool, or silk upholstery. Foam smells musty inside or stays damp (structural mildew). Mildew returns within days of home cleaning (moisture source not fixed). 2 home cycles without progress. Visible mold on the frame or backing. Health concerns — anyone in the home with asthma, immune issues, or mold sensitivity.
escalation.whatToTell: "Mildew on [W/WS/S/X/unknown] upholstery, [synthetic / wool / silk / vintage / mixed], [surface only / suspect structural — foam smells musty], [single spot / multiple cushions / whole piece], age [days / weeks / months], moisture source [identified — humidity / leak / unknown], home-treated with [PPE + outdoor HEPA + cool OxiClean damp-cloth + 3% peroxide on whites + sun-dry] — no chlorine bleach, no saturation. [Recurring within days / not improving / suspect mold inside cushion]."
escalation.specialistType: professional upholstery cleaning service for surface mildew on water-safe fabric; licensed mold remediation specialist if structural mildew, foam saturation, visible mold on frame, or recurring contamination

contextQuestions: Care code — W, S, WS, X, or unknown?
Surface mildew on the cushion cover, or does the foam smell musty inside?
Removable washable cover or fixed upholstery?
Where is the moisture coming from — humidity, leak, condensation?
Anyone in the home with asthma, mold sensitivity, or immune concerns?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Care code is the first gate — W/WS proceed, S/X/unknown to pro. Removable washable covers tolerate the protocol better than fixed upholstery. Foam saturation is the silent failure mode — wet foam grows mildew inside the cushion within days. Moisture-source fix is what makes the cleaning durable. Health-sensitive household = pro / remediation regardless of fabric.
